truths.truth: Activated sludge uses microorganisms to consume biodegradable organic matter.
Return activated sludge maintains biomass concentration in the aeration basin.
Waste activated sludge controls sludge age.
High F/M promotes young sludge conditions.
Low F/M promotes older sludge conditions.
Bulking sludge settles poorly because filamentous organisms interfere with compaction.
Pin floc causes fine solids carryover in the effluent.
Toxic chemicals can inhibit microbial activity and worsen effluent quality.
Cold temperatures slow biological activity in activated sludge systems.
